reason:
CLOSE-OUT REASON (Set 088, session 1)

Operator-authorized close of a doc-only capture set past a spurious blocking
verdict. Cross-provider verification ran normally via the API path (it did NOT
run out of band) and returned ISSUES_FOUND across two rounds; the operator
adjudicated both findings as verifier CATEGORY ERRORS, not defects, and
authorized this close. Verdict recorded WAIVED.

MANUAL-VERIFY ATTESTATION

- Verifying surface: ai_router `verify_session` (routed API), rounds 1 and 2.
- Verifier model: gpt-5-4; effective provider: openai — DISTINCT from the
  orchestrator's effective provider (anthropic / claude-opus-4-8), i.e. the
  same cross-provider exclusion the automated path enforces was satisfied.
- Template: the canonical adversarial `session-verification` template (the
  standard verify_session route; not a hand-rolled review).
- Timestamp: 2026-07-10 (round 1 and round 2 run this date).
- Raw verification artifacts: docs/session-sets/088-external-remediation-
  documentation/s1-verification.md and s1-verification-round-2.md (with
  s1-issues.json / s1-issues-round-2.json).

ADJUDICATION (why WAIVED, not remediated)

Both blocking findings are process/verifier category errors with no defect in
the delivered work (the remediation doc, the 088 spec, and the
repository-reference pointer):

1. Circular "the set is not closed yet" (no change-log.md, status in-progress,
   uncommitted) — unsatisfiable at verify time, because verify_session (Step 6)
   necessarily precedes close-out (Step 8+). Raised round 1, resurrected
   verbatim round 2.
2. "Round-1 verification artifact is stale/false" — it is an immutable,
   append-only point-in-time record; round 2 correctly appended sibling
   artifacts after the one legitimate gap (missing feedback evidence) was
   fixed. "Superseded by a later round" is the append-only system working as
   designed, not falsification; editing the round-1 artifact would itself
   violate the raw-record rule.

Full adjudication: docs/session-sets/088-external-remediation-documentation/
s1-adjudication.md. Per the bounded-round + no-resurrection discipline the loop
stopped at round 2 and escalated to the operator, who accepted the adjudication
(interactive selection, 2026-07-10) and authorized this operator-attested close.
